data.title: Coinbase launches ETH-backed loans as onchain lending tops $1.25B

data.title: Warren Davidson pushes bill to let Americans pay taxes in Bitcoin
data.description:
U. S. Congressman Warren Davidson has introduced an act that would allow Americans to pay federal tax obligations using Bitcoin. The legislation, titled the Bitcoin For America Act, outlines a scheme enabling taxpayers to transfer Bitcoin directly to the U. S. Treasury, with the collected assets directed into a newly established Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. The initiative aims […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/warren-davidson-to-americans-pay-taxes-in-bitcoin/

data.title: US banks can now hold crypto – but why is it only for gas fees?
data.description:
An unnamed national bank has asked the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency for permission to hold crypto on its own balance sheet to support blockchain-based services. On Nov. 18, the OCC finally answered
source: https://cryptoslate.com/us-banks-can-now-hold-crypto-but-only-for-network-fees/

data.title: Bitcoin News Today: Bitcoin Downtrend Deepens as Price Falls to $87K, but Traders Target Short Liquidity at $100K
data.description: Bitcoin’s decline accelerated Thursday, with the price slipping to a six-month low before stabilizing near $87,491, yet traders increasingly expect a sharp upside squeeze toward the $98,000–$100,000 liquidity pocket.Despite bearish technical signals, market participants say a reclaim of key resistance zones could trigger a momentum reversal — though the burden remains squarely on the bulls.Key TakeawaysBitcoin is struggling to hold the $90,000 level, dropping to a six-month low of $88,267.Traders eye short liquidity between $98,000–$100,000, which could fuel a sharp squeeze.BTC lost major long-term support, including the 50-week EMA near $100,000 and the yearly open at $93,300.Analysts warn that $94K, $97K, and $98.5K are the critical levels to reclaim before sentiment turns bullish again.Over $2.1 billion in ask liquidity sits near $96,600–$98,500, creating conditions for a potential rapid upside move.Bitcoin Must Reclaim $98K to Flip Momentum BullishBitcoin has been trending downward since Nov. 3, with consecutive support failures reinforcing bearish sentiment. The breakdown below the:50-week EMA (~$100K)Yearly open ($93,300)has led traders to debate whether the 2025 bull market is over or whether Bitcoin is forming a macro bottom.Swissblock: BTC Is at “Cycle-Level Exhaustion”Private wealth manager Swissblock noted BTC hovering near $90K, saying momentum readings have hit the same exhaustion levels seen:during the March bottomafter the Oct. 10 liquidation cascade“This is where bottoms build,” Swissblock said.“Reclaiming $97K–$98.5K flips momentum constructive. Failing to do so hands bears full control.”Glassnode: $95K–$97K Is Local ResistanceGlassnode analysts identified $95,000–$97,000 as a major barrier, where the -1 STD short-term holder (STH) cost basis currently sits.“Reclaiming it would signal early market equilibrium,” Glassnode wrote.Just above spot price, Bitcoin must break:$94,000 (“local high”)a 290,300-BTC cluster of realized cost basis — a heavy demand zone per Glassnode’s Heatmapto neutralize the current downtrend.The $98K Liquidity Magnet: Will It Pull BTC Higher?While downside risk remains elevated, several traders are focused on the heavy short liquidity stacked between $96,600 and $98,500.According to CoinGlass:More than $2.1 billion in ask orders sit in this zone.BTC is currently “eating away” at liquidity around $92,000.If Bitcoin breaks $98,000, analysts say a short squeeze to $100,000 becomes increasingly likely. AlphaBTC: “$100K–$104K Is a Juicy Target”Bitcoin trader AlphaBTC highlighted the liquidity pocket above $100K:“If $98K breaks, a squeeze to $100K–$104K is on the table.Let’s see if $BTC attempts a move up there before Thanksgiving.”ETF Flows Offer Early Signs of StabilizationAs reported earlier, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $75 million in inflows on Wednesday, ending a five-day streak of outflows.This bounce in flows:eased some downside pressure,hints at early stabilization in institutional demand,and may support attempts to reclaim key resistance levels.Bottom LineBitcoin remains under heavy technical and macro pressure, with the trend firmly pointing downward unless bulls reclaim $94K, $97K, and ultimately $98.5K.But with billions in liquidity overhead — and ETF inflows showing early recovery — traders say a violent short squeeze toward $100K remains a very real possibility if BTC can break out of its current range.

data.title: Bitcoin News: Bitcoin Slumps to $86K, Nearing ‘Max Pain’ Zone but Entering Deep Discount Territory, Analysts Say
data.description: Bitcoin’s slide to $86,000 this week has pushed the market closer to what analysts describe as the “max pain” capitulation zone — a range where large institutional cost bases converge and forced selling risk peaks. While the downturn has rattled sentiment, some analysts argue Bitcoin is entering one of the best long-term discount zones of the cycle.Key TakeawaysAnalysts place Bitcoin’s “max pain” zone between $84,000 and $73,000.BlackRock’s IBIT cost basis (~$84K) and MicroStrategy/Strategy’s (~$73K) define major liquidation and sentiment thresholds.IBIT saw $523 million in single-day outflows, contributing to $3.3B monthly ETF redemptions.Stablecoin reserves hit a record $72B, historically a bullish precursor.Fed uncertainty over December rate cuts continues to pressure liquidity.Analysts: Bitcoin Nearing the Cycle’s “Max Pain” RangeAccording to Bitwise head of research Europe, André Dragosch, Bitcoin’s most likely capitulation window sits between two critical institutional cost-basis levels:BlackRock’s IBIT cost basis: ~$84,000Strategy/MicroStrategy treasury cost basis: ~$73,000Dragosch said a final cycle bottom is statistically most likely to form between these levels, calling the range a “fire-sale zone” representing full market reset and maximum forced selling potential.IBIT’s Role in Setting Market FloorsBlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) — the largest spot BTC ETF — carries a cost basis near $84K.When BTC trades near this level, ETF investors:Begin evaluating whether to redeemReduce net inflowsIncrease liquidity pressure across the marketThis is already visible as IBIT posted a record $523 million outflow on Tuesday, contributing to $3.3 billion in total redemptions over the past month — equal to 3.5% of total AUM.Strategy’s Cost Basis at $73K Creates a Lower “Pain Point”Strategy, the public Bitcoin treasury company modeled after MicroStrategy, is behaving as a secondary anchor for Bitcoin’s downside.Its NAV recently fell below 1, meaning shares now trade at a discount to their underlying BTC — a classic sign of waning liquidity.A retest of $73,000, its treasury cost basis, would historically trigger heavier deleveraging.Analysts warn that if macro conditions deteriorate, the $73K–$74K zone could become a stress point for institutional buyers.Macro Risk Builds as Fed Rate-Cut Odds CollapseAccording to CryptoQuant, uncertainty heading into the December Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting is “exceptionally elevated” due to:The six-week delay in labor data caused by the government shutdownA divided committee on rate cuts vs. inflation risksStalling progress on easing despite persistent ~3% inflationRate-cut odds for December have fallen to 41.8% — down sharply from earlier expectations near 90%.Why This Matters for BitcoinWhen the Fed signals prolonged higher rates:Liquidity tightensETF inflows weakenLeverage resets lowerBitcoin declines sharplyThis is the same environment that triggered Bitcoin’s early-November selloff.Stablecoin Reserves Hit Record $72B — A Silent Bullish SignalDespite the gloomy price action, analysts note a major bullish divergence:Stablecoin exchange reserves are at an all-time high of $72 billion, according to CryptoQuant.Historically:Rising stablecoin reserves → latent buying power,Which has preceded every major Bitcoin rally in 2025,Including the April, July, and early-October surges.If liquidity re-enters the market, these reserves could act as “rocket fuel” for a recovery — but only once macro clarity improves.Price Outlook: $60K, $73K, or Rebound?Under current conditions, analysts map out the following expectations:Base Case (Most Likely):BTC trades between $60,000 and $80,000 through year-end while markets await Fed clarity.Max Pain Scenario:A wick into the $84K–$73K range to test the IBIT + Strategy cost bases.Ultra Bear Case:A breakdown toward $60K, especially if ETF outflows worsen or the Fed turns more hawkish.Bull Case (Low Probability Until Fed Pivot):A strong liquidity rebound and ETF inflows that push BTC back into the $94K–$101K range.Bottom LineBitcoin’s fall to $86K has brought the market closer to its historically powerful max pain and discount zone, where institutional cost bases cluster and long-term value emerges.While macro risks remain elevated and liquidity is tight, rising stablecoin reserves and heavy capitulation signals suggest that Bitcoin may be approaching the final phase of its corrective cycle — though volatility is likely to intensify before any meaningful recovery begins.

data.title: Ethereum Is Sitting On Its Most Critical On-Chain Support Level — A Rally Emerging?
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market finds itself at a compelling crossroads, and Ethereum has once again returned to a pivotal on-chain support zone that has historically marked major turning points in its market cycle. With ETH now pressing against this same support, the market is exhibiting a strong reaction that could confirm the integrity of the long-term trend. Why This On-Chain Support Zone Defined Ethereum Recovery Ethereum’s price is sitting right on top of its most important on-chain support, and the behavior around this level is exactly what occurs before a big reversal. A popular crypto news site, CryptosRus, has revealed on X that the ETH price sharply dropped to $2,870 earlier today, only to bounce instantly after Nvidia’s earnings lifted the entire market
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/ethereum/ethereum-key-on-chain-support/

data.title: Bitcoin News: Bitcoin Crashes to $86,600 Before Rebounding to $87,213 as Extreme Fear Grips the Market
data.description: Bitcoin plunged to $86,610 early Thursday — its lowest level in seven months — before rebounding sharply to $87,213, as extreme fear and macro uncertainty continue to shake crypto markets. The rebound comes after a volatile overnight session driven by strong tech earnings and fresh U.S. labor market data.Bitcoin Price: New 7-Month Low Then a Stabilizing ReboundBitcoin briefly traded above $92,000 overnight before a steep early-morning selloff dragged the price down to $86,610.The top cryptocurrency has since stabilized, trading at $87,213, up slightly from the session lows.Latest metrics:Current BTC price: $87,21324-hour low: $86,61024-hour change: −1%24-hour trading volume: $87 billionMarket cap: $1.78 trillion (−1% on the day)Circulating supply: 19,950,600 BTCFear & Greed Index: Extreme FearBitcoin is now 5% below its seven-day high of $92,944 and printing fresh seven-day and seven-month lows.Macro Backdrop: Strong Payrolls, Rising UnemploymentThe U.S. labor market showed unexpected strength in September as the Bureau of Labor Statistics released delayed jobs data after the government shutdown.Key numbers:+119,000 jobs added (vs. 50,000 expected)Unemployment rate: 4.4% (up from 4.3%)August: revised to a 4,000-job lossThis data set — normally released in early October — is the first official economic update in more than six weeks, with the next release expected in mid-December.The mixed results reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve is unlikely to cut rates in December, maintaining pressure on risk assets.Markets Lifted Overnight by Nvidia’s Blockbuster EarningsBitcoin’s brief move above $92,000 came after Nvidia’s better-than-expected earnings eased investor concerns about an AI-market slowdown.Nvidia reported:$57 billion in revenueStrong Q4 guidanceSurging demand for AI infrastructureGlobal markets rallied:Nasdaq futures: +1.9%S&P 500 futures: +1%Asian equities: broadly higher10-year yield: 4.11%U.S. dollar: modest gainsAI-linked liquidity remains a key driver for crypto, and Nvidia’s results signaled robust tech-sector investment ahead.ETF Flows Show Signs of Recovery After $3B OutflowsBitcoin’s decline this month coincided with more than $3 billion in U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF outflows, which helped drag BTC toward the $87K region.On Wednesday, however, ETF flows turned positive:+$75 million inflows, per DefiLlamaThis helped support Bitcoin’s rebound back above $87,000.Bitcoin Price OutlookBitcoin closed last week at $94,290, decisively breaking below the $96,000 support level — a critical structural threshold for the 2025 bull cycle.Losing $96K marked a shift in sentiment, with bears regaining full control.Supports to Watch1. $83,000–$84,000 (major Fibonacci support)Aligned with the 0.382 retracement from the 2022 bottom to the 2025 high.2. $69,000–$72,000 (2024 consolidation range)If BTC breaks below $83K, this zone becomes the next likely landing area.Resistance AheadEven with the rebound:Immediate resistance: $94,000–$98,000Short-squeeze target: $101,000Major resistance band: $106,000–$109,000Macro resistance: $114,000–$116,000Analysts say a close above $116,000 would be required to flip market structure firmly bullish again.Sentiment Remains Extremely BearishBitcoin has fallen more than 25% from its October peak, with analysts noting the broadening wedge pattern still signals a bearish continuation unless BTC reclaims higher levels.Best-case scenario:A short rally to $106,000 before facing renewed selling pressure.Base-case scenario:A retest of the $83K–$84K support zone.Crypto markets remain heavily macro-driven, leaving Bitcoin vulnerable to further volatility.

data.title: 3 Reasons Why Bitcoin and Crypto Are Dumping Today: $112 Billion Erased in 3 Hours
data.description:
Here’s an expanded version of your sentence: 

"Today, the cryptocurrency market is experiencing a significant downturn, with Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies witnessing a sharp decline in value. In just three hours, an astonishing $112 billion has been wiped off the total market capitalization. Here are three key reasons contributing to this sudden sell-off in digital assets."
